headline: James Webb Telescope Unveils Secrets of an Extreme Lava Planet
meta_description: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope reveals a hydrogen-rich atmosphere on 55 Cancri e, a lava planet where rock melts and volcanic gases shape a strange sky.
summary_short: The James Webb Space Telescope has mapped the atmosphere of a scorching lava planet, discovering hydrogen-rich gases and evidence of volcanic clouds in an utterly alien…
body:
NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has peered into the brutally extreme atmosphere of 55 Cancri e, a lava planet where temperatures soar high enough to melt solid rock. The observations reveal a world far stranger than scientists expected, with an atmosphere rich in hydrogen shaped by gases escaping from the planet's molten interior.

The data suggests something even more remarkable: volcanic outgassing may create temporary clouds in this hellish environment. These findings challenge assumptions about what planetary atmospheres can be and how extreme worlds might behave.

Each discovery from the James Webb Telescope expands our understanding of the cosmos and the diversity of worlds beyond our solar system. This glimpse into an alien atmosphere shows just how much there is to learn about planetary science.
